title: Fazmotrons Japan Eva Trip
wikitext:
[]: Fazmotron's Japan Eva Trip             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Thu Jan 19, 2012 3:12 am Sup Geeks. Because I have so much Evangelion stuff to show you from my trip to Japan I've decided to dump it all into this thread, starting with my visits to the Evangelion Store at Harajuku in Tokyo. I made this video while I have there, it is a complete walkthrough of the store and of most of the stuff they are selling there. (The only bit of the store not in the video is the art wall that has sketches and artworks from various artists, including a signed sketch by Hideaki Anno). The Eva-Store is just off the main strip of shops that is directly across from Harajuku train station. (A street that is almost constantly packed with people) The Store is 2 stories of nearly every type of Eva merchandise you can think of, and some you would never logically think of. Like this series of horse/pony/Eva things. The bottom floor is all the really expensive stuff like jewellery and clothing, and upstairs is all the other general merchandise like books and cards and toys and so on. Upstairs has a large display wall filled with all sorts of Eva figures, postcards, dinnerware and so on. Around the back of the store there is an Eva vending machine, gashapon station, and the wall of Q characters. And here's the rest of the photos of the store, including close ups of the character wall. [URL=http://imageshack.us/photo/my-images/864/dscf1021v.jpg/] [/URL][URL=http://imageshack.us/photo/my-images/607/dscf0896m.jpg/] [/URL][URL=http://imageshack.us/photo/my-images/99/dscf1020i.jpg/] [/URL][URL=http://imageshack.us/photo/my-images/221/dscf1019k.jpg/] [/URL] [URL=http://imageshack.us/photo/my-images/59/dscf1013l.jpg/] [/URL][URL=http://imageshack.us/photo/my-images/822/dscf1014jo.jpg/] [/URL][URL=http://imageshack.us/photo/my-images/403/dscf1015z.jpg/] [/URL][URL=http://imageshack.us/photo/my-images/688/dscf1016s.jpg/] [/URL][URL=http://imageshack.us/photo/my-images/406/dscf1017t.jpg/] [/URL] Coming Soon: All the stuff I bought (16kg worth), and a video walkthrough of Evangelion World at Fuji-Q Highland.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Thu Jan 19, 2012 4:22 am "Sup Geeks" Fazmatron, I'd like to thank you for going out of your way to not only visit the store, but to record your walk through the store on a handheld camera to share with us. It means a lot to me because I have no certainty if I'll ever visit Japan, or if the store will be still the same. Great video, great photographs, and I look forward to seeing your next post.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Thu Jan 19, 2012 5:50 am So jelly!

[]:             Quote Post by insomniac » Thu Jan 19, 2012 8:19 am Cool wallets! And so many other cool stuff!

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Jan 19, 2012 8:59 am Great stuff, Fazmotron. I will go to Tokyo later this year and surely would pay it a visit. One thing to note: How easy you can spot female visitors in the store. I guess locating Eva Store in Harajuku (which has hordes of female shoppers) is a wise choice (compare to locating it in Akiharaba for example).

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Thu Jan 19, 2012 9:18 am Those are all some really great pictures

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Thu Jan 19, 2012 11:31 am Thanks for the pix, Faz.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Thu Jan 19, 2012 12:24 pm Faz: excellent work, man. You could try to add a watermark to make sure all the shots are yours.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Thu Jan 19, 2012 1:59 pm Holy cow, these are awesome pics Faz! More Eva merchandise, the merrier.

[]: Re: Fazmotron's Japan Eva Trip             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jan 19, 2012 2:43 pm Fazmotron wrote: Like this series of horse/pony/Eva things.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Thu Jan 19, 2012 5:11 pm Niiice.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Thu Jan 19, 2012 6:12 pm I like how they have Kaji's watermelon patch, is the one in the photo actually a real watermelon plant or fake one for decoration? Fazmotron wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Thu Jan 19, 2012 7:50 pm Great pictures! I definitely want to go to this store one day if I get the chance. Even if I don't buy anything it would still be a lot of fun. That being said, I know that Eva merchandise can be expensive, but is that an 8000 yen shot glass I'm looking at? Someone please tell me there is a misunderstanding here.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Thu Jan 19, 2012 9:28 pm soul.assassin wrote: You could try to add a watermark to make sure all the shots are yours.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Jan 19, 2012 9:34 pm rhfxz1s wrote: That being said, I know that Eva merchandise can be expensive, but is that an 8000 yen shot glass I'm looking at? Someone please tell me there is a misunderstanding here.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Thu Jan 19, 2012 9:40 pm What are those Sachiel thingy at 2:50?

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Thu Jan 19, 2012 9:55 pm KnightmareX13 wrote: Are these watches?

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Thu Jan 19, 2012 10:41 pm Are they still selling the bucket ? Tokyo Anime Center at Akihabara UDX had a few in stock last time I was there.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Jan 19, 2012 10:59 pm ^ Actually it looks kind of cool. I don't see why it is silly . Overpriced perhaps, but not silly. Imagine you can kick the bucket with Evangelion; or do gardening like Kaji with the bucket, etc etc...

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Thu Jan 19, 2012 11:43 pm symbv wrote: Imagine you can kick the bucket with Evangelion...
